content: Self-Refine’s iterative output refinement and Reflexion’s dynamic memory-enabled self-reflection can be combined to form a two-layer autonomous self-improvement system for LLM agents: Self-Refine handles task-specific output polishing in the short term, while Reflexion accumulates cross-task reflection patterns into long-term dynamic memory to guide more effective self-feedback for future Self-Refine iterations. This system retains the key advantage of both approaches—requiring no additional supervised training or external data—while closing the gap between single-task refinement and generalized self-improvement.
